---
title: Transformation
bibliography: references.bib
format:
  html:
    css: /style/page_style.css
---

<span class="source-note">Sources: @hyndman_toolbox, @monks_arima</span>

Mathematical transformations are used to simplify the patterns in historical data to make forecasting simpler and more accurate. Any forecasts are produced on a transformed scale, so the data then has to be back-transformed.

For example, if variation increases or decreases over time, a logarithmic transformation is often usedful. If log base 10 is used, then an increase of 1 on the log scale corresponds to a multiplication of 10 on the original scale.

## References

::: {#refs}
:::
